project_reference.core_research_question: Does a real material exist (or can one be designed) with lambda_ph >= 3.0, d-wave pairing symmetry (mu*=0), and omega_log_eff >= 740 K -- the three conditions needed for Tc = 300 K?
project_reference.current_focus: Phase 74 -- Orbital-Selective Candidate Survey (Track A entry) / Phase 76 -- Superlattice Interface Design (Track B entry) / Phase 78 -- Frustrated Magnet Survey (Track C entry)

scope.question: After v5.0, which route family gives the best chance of actually shrinking the remaining 149 K gap: Hg-family uplift around the Hg1223 benchmark, nickelate escalation via pressure, strain, and chemical pressure, or neither?
in_scope: Current primary-source route comparison across Hg-family cuprates, nickelates, and conventional near-ambient controls
Explicit comparison of Tc headroom, operating pressure, retention status, and controllable uplift levers
Ranking the next route-expansion target for post-v5.0 work
out_of_scope: Claiming a room-temperature finished material before decisive evidence exists
Treating a pressure-only or onset-only signal as an ambient practical route
Device engineering or consumer hardware design
unresolved_questions: Does the Hg cuprate family still offer the best absolute Tc headroom after v5.0 once pressure and multilayer physics are taken seriously together?
Are nickelates now improving fast enough to justify promotion from backup to a more active route-expansion target?
Which route class should the repo pursue next if the goal is to shrink the remaining 149 K gap rather than only improve execution discipline?

user_asserted_anchors: Do not confuse a benchmark or research platform with a consumer-ready room-temperature material
The best carried retained benchmark remains 149 K below room temperature
known_good_baselines: Hydride negative baseline: CsInH3 reaches 214 K at 3 GPa but ambient retention is unsupported
Current retained benchmark baseline: Hg1223 reaches up to 151 K after pressure quench
Nickelate frontier baseline: ambient film onset near 63 K, ambient bulk around 40 K, and pressurized single-crystal reports up to 96 K
context_gaps: No current route is close to room-temperature ambient operation
The transfer from higher-pressure Hg-family headroom to retained ambient operation is unproven
The best next route program after v5.0 is not yet fixed
crucial_inputs: Keep pressure-history and operating-pressure language separate in every claim
Reject route promotion based only on onset-only or pressure-only evidence
Keep the room-temperature gap explicit in all route updates

formulations: Common-basis route comparison instead of isolated Tc headlines
Named control-lever mapping rather than vague route enthusiasm
Explicit survivor-set screening before the next route program is chosen
forbidden_estimator_families: Treating pressure-only records as ambient-ready routes
Promoting theory-only near-ambient optimism as if it were benchmark-level evidence
Calling route expansion a room-temperature breakthrough
allowed_fit_families: Route ranking tied to headroom, operating state, evidence depth, and control richness
Shortlists that name one primary and one secondary route
forbidden_fit_families: Diffuse route watchlists with no priority order
Room-temperature extrapolation from the carried frontier values
stop_and_rethink_conditions: The frontier map cannot identify a route with meaningfully better upside than the carried v5.0 baseline
Phase 23 still cannot choose one primary and one secondary route
Any route update hides the 149 K room-temperature gap

observables.name: Route benchmark Tc
observables.definition: Best current benchmark temperature associated with a route family

observables.name: Operating state
observables.definition: Whether the benchmark route is retained ambient, ambient-native, or pressure-only

observables.name: Evidence depth
observables.definition: How strong the current evidence is for the benchmark route

observables.name: Control richness
observables.definition: Number and quality of named knobs that can plausibly move the route frontier

observables.name: Room-temperature gap
observables.definition: Difference between 300 K and the best carried retained Tc benchmark

claims.statement: The project can define a current frontier headroom map that compares Hg-family cuprates, nickelates, and conventional near-ambient controls on one honest basis.
The project can convert the Phase 22 frontier map into a route-expansion program with one primary route and one secondary route.

deliverables.description: Frontier headroom map for the surviving route families
Control-ledger for the serious route families
Negative-control screening note for weak route classes
Shortlist of the next route classes and candidate families

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-frontier-headroom-map
acceptance_tests.procedure: Inspect the Phase 22 route comparison outputs.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Routes are compared on headroom, operating state, and evidence depth together, and weak classes are screened explicitly.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-route-expansion-program
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The repo ends with one primary and one secondary route while keeping the room-temperature gap explicit.

references.why_it_matters: Keeps pressure-history and operating-pressure language separate.
Anchors the higher-pressure headroom of the Hg family.
Anchors the ambient nickelate film frontier.
Anchors the current highest nickelate frontier.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-frontier-headroom-map
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Ranking a pressure-only route as if it already operated at ambient pressure
forbidden_proxies.reason: That would erase the distinction between headroom and practical operation.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-route-expansion-program
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Ending the route-expansion pass with a diffuse watchlist and no primary route
forbidden_proxies.reason: That would fail to narrow the route problem after v5.0.

weakest_anchors: No current route is close to room-temperature ambient operation.
Higher-pressure headroom may not transfer to retained ambient operation.
unvalidated_assumptions: The next milestone can exploit the identified headroom rather than only describe it.
competing_explanations: The best route program may still be Hg1223 reproduction support rather than route expansion.
disconfirming_observations: If Phase 23 still cannot choose one primary and one secondary route, v6.0 has not narrowed the route problem enough.

position.last_activity_desc: v14.0 roadmap created; three parallel tracks ready to plan

intermediate_results: v1.0 pipeline benchmarks: H3S Tc = 182 K (10.5% error vs expt), LaH10 Tc = 276 K (10.6% error vs expt)
v1.0 CsInH3 result: Tc = 214 K at 3 GPa (SSCHA-corrected), E_hull = 6 meV/atom
v8.0 phonon-only Tc ceiling: 26-36 K for all oxide candidates
v8.0 mechanism analysis: phonon fraction 20-45% of cuprate Tc; spin fluctuations contribute 55-80%
v8.0 Hg multilayer finding: n=3 (Hg1223) is optimally layered
v9.0 DMFT: Z=0.33, m*/m=3.0, Mott proximity confirmed for Hg1223
v9.0 Spin susceptibility: lambda_sf=1.8 (single-site), d-wave channel dominant
v9.0 Spectral gate: PASS 3/4 (pseudogap, Hubbard bands, d-wave)
v9.0 Full Eliashberg Tc: 108 K for Hg1223 (-28% vs 151 K experimental)
v10.0 DCA Nc=4: Z_nodal=0.195, Z_antinodal=0.054; lambda_sf_cluster=2.88 (Hubbard-I)
v10.0 best prediction: 242 K [200, 300] for Hg1223 strained+15 GPa (Hubbard-I; overestimate)
**v11.0 CTQMC correction: lambda_sf dropped 33% (2.88 -> 1.92); Hubbard-I overestimate confirmed**
**v11.0 Nc-convergence: lambda_sf_inf = 2.70 via extrapolation; strong-coupling saturation**
**v11.0 best prediction: 146 K [106, 216] for Hg1223 -- within 3% of experimental 151 K**
**v11.0 Tc ceiling: ~200 K with known spin-fluctuation + phonon physics**
**v11.0 bottleneck identified: omega_log ~400 K for cuprates caps the ceiling**
**v11.0 key insight: hydrogen modes (omega_log ~1000-2000 K) + d-wave mu*=0 + lambda_sf ~2-3 could shift ceiling to ~400 K**
**v12.0 Allen-Dynes omega_log_eff = 483 K with best candidate: lambda_ph=1.27, omega_ph=852 K, lambda_sf=2.23, omega_sf=350 K**
**v12.0 Tc = 197 K at mu*=0 (Allen-Dynes) -- 103 K short of 300 K**
**v12.0 target: omega_log_eff = 740 K needed at lambda_total=3.5 for Tc = 300 K**
**v12.0 three routes identified: (A) stiffer SF, (B) anisotropic Eliashberg, (C) phonon-dominant**
**v13.0 finding: 300 K requires lambda_ph >= 3.0 + d-wave (mu*=0) + omega_log_eff >= 740 K**
**v13.0 key obstacle: d-wave requires correlations (large U) but correlations produce SF that drag omega_log_eff down**
**v13.0 three strategies for v14.0: orbital-selective, interface proximity, frustrated magnetism + H**
Carried retained benchmark: Hg1223 at 151 K after pressure quench (ref-hg1223-quench)
Room-temperature gap: 149 K (unchanged since v4.0); computational gap: 103 K (from v12.0 best)
open_questions: Can orbital-selective Mott physics truly decouple the d-wave channel from the phonon channel?
Does proximity coupling preserve d-wave symmetry in the phonon-active layer, or does it revert to s-wave?
Can geometric frustration suppress lambda_sf while preserving enough total coupling (lambda_total >= 3.0)?
Is the lambda_ph=3 + d-wave material fundamentally forbidden by a no-go theorem, or just hard to find?
Is the Migdal theorem valid when omega_log approaches 800+ K?

decisions.summary: [v11.0 Phase 57]: Known spin-fluctuation + phonon physics caps Tc at ~200 K; omega_log ~400 K is the cuprate bottleneck
[v12.0 start]: Hydrogen-correlated oxide inverse design -- combine hydride omega_log with cuprate spin fluctuations and d-wave Coulomb evasion
[v12.0 closeout]: Allen-Dynes omega_log_eff = 483 K yields Tc = 197 K; 103 K gap to 300 K remains; three routes to close it identified

[v13.0 start]: Three parallel tracks to close 103 K gap: (A) high-J materials with omega_sf > 500 K, (B) full anisotropic Eliashberg to beat Allen-Dynes, (C) phonon-dominant design with weak SF + high omega_eff
[v13.0 closeout]: 300 K requires lambda_ph >= 3.0 + d-wave (mu*=0) + omega_log_eff >= 740 K; the central tension is that d-wave needs correlations but correlations produce SF drag
[v14.0 start]: Hybrid Material Design -- three strategies to resolve the correlation/phonon tension: orbital-selective, interface proximity, frustrated magnetism + hydrogen
Started v15.0: Beyond-Eliashberg mechanisms for 300 K
decisions.rationale: v14.0 proved Eliashberg ceiling at 240 K. Need non-adiabatic, plasmon, excitonic, or unknown mechanism to reach 300 K.

approximations.name: Allen-Dynes formula
approximations.status: v12.0 baseline; anisotropic Eliashberg preferred in v14.0

convention_lock.fourier_convention: QE plane-wave: `psi_nk = e^{ikr} u_nk`; asymmetric `1/Omega` normalization

custom_conventions.custom: SI-derived reporting (K, GPa, eV, meV); pressure in GPa; `e > 0`, electron charge `-e`

blockers: Orbital selectivity may not genuinely decouple phonon and SF channels -- interorbital hybridization could mix them
Proximity effect typically reduces the stronger layer's Tc rather than enhancing it
Frustrated magnets may lose d-wave pairing along with spin fluctuations (both come from the same AF exchange)
Migdal theorem validity remains unverified for omega_log > 800 K
All three tracks may fail: the lambda_ph=3 + d-wave material may not exist within known chemistry

session.stopped_at: v14.0 roadmap created; Phases 74, 76, 78 ready to plan (parallel entry)
